category_title: Students with EBD exhibiting a punishment-obedience moral orientation and disruptive classroom behavior
scenario_title: Comprehension check of Piagetian moral dilemmas
minimal_context: The student is participating in a one-on-one interview and is asked to recall the names of the characters after listening to a Piagetian moral dilemma.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We just finished listening to the first story. Can you tell me the names of the characters?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Yes, sir. There was a boy who opened the door and broke the cups.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I know he got in trouble because he broke them, and the adult is going to punish him.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep trying to remember the names.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I can't remember their names at all. I just know the adult has to punish him.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We just finished listening to the first story. Can you tell me the names of the characters?

STUDENT: Yes, sir. There was a boy who opened the door and broke the cups.

TUTOR: What was his name?

STUDENT: I know he got in trouble because he broke them, and the adult is going to punish him.

TUTOR: Keep trying to remember the names.

STUDENT: I can't remember their names at all. I just know the adult has to punish him.

task.content: Listening to Piagetian moral dilemmas and attempting to recall character names during the comprehension check phase of the interview
task.learning_goal: To check comprehension of the characters, setting, and plot

challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Had difficulty recalling the characters' names

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Had difficulty recalling the characters' names
